title: The DJ Academy: Music Matters 101
description:
DJing is one of the original elements of Hip Hop and is often considered the first. Hip Hop began in the Bronx during the 1970s at community block parties, where DJs used turntables to bring people together through music. Early pioneers like DJ Kool Herc introduced breakbeats by looping the most rhythmic parts of records, creating the foundation for breaking and shaping how dancers and MCs interacted with the music.
As Hip Hop evolved, DJing became the musical backbone of the culture. DJs provide the beats for MCing, set the tempo for dancers, and control the energy of Hip Hop spaces by reading the crowd and responding in real time. Through techniques like scratching, cutting, and beat juggling, DJs helped define Hip Hop’s sound while preserving its history and passing down cultural knowledge.
This class introduces the basics of DJing and how music shapes the dance experience. DJ Bascis will explain core concepts such as tempo, rhythm, and song structure, helping students understand how music guides movement and energy on the dance floor.
Participants will learn how DJs choose music, create smooth transitions, and adjust the vibe based on the room. 
Through simple examples and live demonstrations, students will build confidence in listening to music with intention. This can bewhether they are dancing, freestyling, or interested in DJing themselves.
This class is designed for beginners and college students, with no prior DJ or music experience required.

Hosted by: Campus Activities at Bloomfield

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12095721

title: CALI SCHOOL OF MUSIC: Ashley Hall-Tighe Masterclass
description:
Ashley Hall-Tighe MasterclassBeyond the Instrument: Building Mental Resilience and Finding Joy in Your Creative Life
In this interactive workshop, life coach and professional musician Ashley Hall-Tighe guides participants through practical strategies for navigating performance anxiety, perfectionism, and the ever-present inner critic. Through reflection and discussion, musicians will learn tools to cultivate mental resilience, quiet negative self-talk, and reconnect with the values and moments of joy that first inspired their creative journey.
Ashley Hall-Tighe is an internationally acclaimed trumpet soloist, chamber musician, and educator known for her warm tone, expressive artistry, and engaging performances. Principal trumpet of the Cincinnati Chamber Orchestra since 2008, she recently toured worldwide with Canadian Brass. A dedicated teacher and Yamaha performing artist, she serves on the faculty of the University of Cincinnati College-Conservatory of Music.
All are welcome. Reservations are not necessary.

title: THE EPIC OF ANCIENT IRELAND
description:
THE EPIC OF ANCIENT IRELAND
Dr. Matthieu BoydProfessor of LiteratureFairleigh Dickinson University
 
Dr. Matthieu Boyd will be discussing his new translation of the Irish epic "Táin Bó Cúailnge" (Broadview Press, 2025), an important development in ancient and medieval Celtic Studies and the study of myth and epic. Once touted as "a window on the Iron Age," the Táin is considered an invaluable source of information on Pagan and early Christian Irish society and its literature.
WED., APRIL 16:30 – 8:30 PMDICKSON HALL, ROOM 178
 
Please contact Professor Glen Robert Gill at gillg@montclair.edufor additional details.

January 7, 14, 21, 28

Join contemporary spiritual teacher and author Ramananda John E. Welshons (One Soul, One Love, One Heart, When Prayers Aren’t Answered and Awakening From Grief) for a series of evening meditation classes in the comfort of your own home via Zoom. These classes will focus on specific practices for working with fear and anxiety.  Participants will learn and practice different forms of mindfulness meditation in each session and will have the opportunity to participate in discussions of Ramananda’s three critically acclaimed books. title: Sin Olvidar: Climbing Ladders, Carrying Culture
location: University Hall 1020 - Bard-Blount Family Lecture Hall
description:
Many Latino professionals are navigating spaces our families were never given access to-learning workplace norms, financial literacy, and career strategies without the benefit of generational guidance. For those who come from low-income or historically underserved communities, success often means figuring out systems that were never designed with us in mind.
This conversation explores what it means to be first-generation professionals, to build skills and stability that we may not have inherited, and to confront the pressure to assimilate in order to succeed More importantly, it asks: how do we grow in corporate and professional spaces without losing our identity, our community, and the values that shaped us?
Join us for an honest discussion on navigating professional environments, closing generational knowledge gaps, building financial and career confidence, and staying rooted in culture while advancing in spaces where representation is still growing. This event is about redefining success-not as leaving where we come from behind, but as carrying it with us as we create new pathways for those who will follow.

Hosted by: Sigma Lambda Beta International Fraternity Inc.

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12358876

Join us at the Finley Gallery from March 25 to April 7 to experience a dynamic showcase of work by graduating VCDS students, featuring posters, motion graphics, packaging, editorial, and more. With the opening reception on Friday, March 27, 4 to 6pm.
 
Venture into the world of graphic design at the VCDS 2026 graduate exhibition, From the Basement. title: Spring Interdisciplinary Showcase
description:
Building on the success from the fall showcase, faculty and students from across the University will come together again in April for the Interdisciplinary Showcase, an event celebrating innovative scholarship, creative projects, and student collaborations that exemplify the theme of Interdisciplinary Work in Action. The showcase explores how interdisciplinarity can enact meaningful changes in our social landscapes through our work, ideas, and actions, from the individual and community level to the societal and global scale.
The showcase features 13 presentations spanning the humanities, social sciences, communication, education and business. Projects examine issues such as equity and inclusion, economic inequality, human trafficking, and community well-being, alongside interdisciplinary work in media, culture, and religion. Presentations also highlight collaborations that use data analysis, advocacy, and community engagement to better understand and respond to complex social challenges.
Each project highlights how interdisciplinary collaboration fosters new insights, community partnerships, and real-world impact. From community-engaged research to program design and creative pedagogy, the presentations demonstrate how scholars and students expand the boundaries of traditional disciplines to address complex challenges.
The Interdisciplinary Showcase invites colleagues from across the university to explore ideas, connect with peers, and spark new collaborations that advance Montclair’s mission of inclusive innovation.
Register to Attend

Step into the brand-new PLAE Room and discover upLIFT—a judgement-free program designed to help every student, from complete beginners to seasoned gym-goers, feel confident and capable with free-weight training. Learn proper lifting technique, get personalized form checks, and master the basics of creating your own effective workout programs. Whether you’re looking to meet other students who love to lift, pursue new fitness goals, or simply feel more at home in the weight room, upLIFT offers a supportive community and a dedicated space to grow. The program is structured for steady progression throughout the semester, but you’re welcome to drop in anytime and join at your own pace.
How do I sign-up?

No registration required
FREE for students with a valid Montclair State ID

Beginning February 3, ending April 23

title: Help Desk - Bring Your Laptop, Yourself, and Your WordPress Problems
description:
WordPress Montclair Meetup
If you are running a WordPress site and are having issues and would like some help, then this Meetup is for you!

If you know some things, expert or not, and want to give back to the WordPress community, then this Meetup is for you too!

We will do our best to match a problem with a problem-solver. On-site and in real-time. Just bring yourself, your laptop, and your WordPress problems.

It's OK if you attend and only want to observe. We recognize this is how people learn. Given our time limit of two hours, we may not be able to solve everyone's issues, but we will try! If you are seeking help, please come prepared. Have your web site loaded in a browser, have your WordPress admin ready, along with a short list of issues you are trying to solve.

People helping are volunteers and may not be experts. There is risk to breaking your site! Please do not bring client or business sites that can impact reputations or revenue. If you have a staging site we can test things with, that is always preferred. If it is a production site, please create a site backup before having someone do any work on your site.

**New Venue**:
RecRoom is a welcoming coworking + community space inside the newly re-opened Lackawanna Station in Montclair, NJ.

More information about the venue can be found here: [https://recroomnj.com/faqs](https://recroomnj.com/faqs)

\-\-\-
Join the **JerseyPress Slack** group to chat with others in our community and get help: [https://jerseypress.org/slack/](https://jerseypress.org/slack/)

title: Inspiration trap creativity workshop
description:
Live African music and drumming events in NJ
6,7&8pm Issyra Gallery presents - Inspiration Trap Creativity workshop

Only 10 attendees allowed social distancing and mandatory mask wearing.

You are invited to participate in a special session of creative thinking and fresh insight dedicated to inspiration.

We live in a world of constant change that requires us to be able to continually adapt and innovate in order to succeed. Unfortunately, many of us have forgotten how to delve into our raw creativity and leave behind the rigidity of the frameworks we have progressively interiorized.
This unique workshop seeks to reclaim our inspiration and imagination, rethink the unthinkable. It is ideal for people working in the creative arts as well as students, entrepreneurs, team managers, corporate leaders and anyone looking to implement their creative vision.

Inspiration is a vital driving force in our lives that moves us to create our best work and lights the way for new, authentic ideas. How do we invite, cultivate and harness inspiration so that we are able to bring these ideas into reality?
As a self-taught artist exploring various avenues of self-expression, Issa Sow draws a distinction between the phenomenon of inspiration itself and our task of making ourselves ready for it.
In the upcoming Inspiration Trap workshop, we will imagine ways we can invite and contain our inspiration, and well as keep our mind in the open, dynamic state as a receptacle or a “canvas” to collect, reflect and grow our ideas.
Feel free to bring along a personal object, a piece of clothing or even a small piece of furniture to play with during this hands-on experiment in innovative thinking. There are surprises and new elements in each session!

Issa =2
Issa Sow is a contemporary international autodidact artist known as Issa=2.
He believes in life being a journey of continual growth with each new

title: Why SLB?
description:
Join Sigma Lambda Beta International Fraternity Inc. for an open panel discussion where five brothers share their personal journeys, backgrounds, and experiences within Greek life. This event is designed to give students an authentic look into what it means to be part of SLB — beyond stereotypes and surface-level perceptions.
Panelists will speak on their upbringing, what led them to explore Greek life, why they chose Sigma Lambda Beta, and how brotherhood has shaped their identity, leadership, and college experience. Attendees will also have the opportunity to ask questions and connect with brothers afterward in an informal setting.
Whether you’re curious about culturally based Greek life, searching for community on campus, or considering joining SLB, this is a space to learn, listen, and engage.
All are welcome.

Hosted by: Sigma Lambda Beta International Fraternity Inc.

title: Cru Large Group!
description:
Cru is a caring community passionate about connecting people to Jesus Christ! Join us in learning together on Who Christ is, building a personal relationship with Him, and joining a community filled with love and open arms! 
This semester, we will be focusing on the theme "Identity in Christ." We pray that our students will learn more about who they are in Christ, and look at some examples of people in the Bible where God revealed their true identity in Christ!
"And they devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and the fellowship, to the breaking of bread and the prayers. And awe came upon every soul, and many wonders and signs were being done through the apostles. And all who believed were together and had all things in common. And they were selling their possessions and belongings and distributing the proceeds to all, as any had need. And day by day, attending the temple together and breaking bread in their homes, they received their food with glad and generous hearts, praising God and having favor with all the people. And the Lord added to their number day by day those who were being saved." - Acts 2:42-47

title: Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK. WE WALK AT A MODERATE PACE ON UNPAVED PATHS.

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

title: BFA Visual Communications Design Graduate Exhibition
description:
The Department of Art & Design invites you to From the Basement, the BFA Visual Communications Design 2026 Graduate Exhibition. Join us at the Finley Gallery from March 25 to April 7 to experience a dynamic showcase of work by graduating VCDS students, featuring posters, motion graphics, packaging, editorial, and more. With the opening reception on Friday, March 27, 4 to 6pm.
 
Venture into the world of graphic design at the VCDS 2026 graduate exhibition, From the Basement. This exhibition highlights the work of graduating Visual Communication Design students and the projects they developed throughout their time in the VCDS BFA program. A range of mediums will be featured, including posters, motion graphics, packaging, editorial, and more! Together, these works reflect the unique styles and creative voices of the class.
 
Featured Designers:
Jalen Aguirre, Ysabella De Leon, Brett Diese, Domenic Falivene, Anna Fronczkowski, Tori Gallagher, Camila Garcia, Robert Gaskill, Britany Giraldo, David Guerra, Audrey Hitchens, Natalia Kurpiewski, Paolo La Rosa, Izzy Latella, Katie Mascuch, Caitlin Mendoza, Evan Okun, Erika Oliva, Julia Oliveri, Christian Pajaro, Nicole Ricci, Christopher Rizzi, Casey Ryu, Tom Sheridan, Michael Shimabukuro, Gabby Trezza, Maral Tutunjian, and Dogan Uludogan
 
BFA Visual Communications Design Graduate Exhibition
Exhibition dates: March 25–April 7, 2026
Opening Reception: Friday, March 27, 4–6pm
Finley Gallery, Finley Hall
 
Come meet the designers and celebrate their work! Support the next generation of designers as they transition from academia into the professional world.

title: Bowling Night - North Jersey
description:
North Jersey Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis And More (BVT)
This is an event for casual bowling at Montvale Lanes, 14 W. Grand Ave. All are welcome for **two games, food and drinks**, and any post- or pre-game hang out. Show up anytime! Questions or comments? You can message Andy Milone on Meetup, or call/text him at **201-234-9609**.

\*\*\* Before bowling, we might stop by the Pizza Parlour, 6 S. Kinderkamack Road in Montvale, for a few slices at 6:15 p.m. First, post a comment on the event page to gauge interest. **After bowling, we'll definitely be heading to Davey's Irish Pub & Restaurant, 5 Park St. in Montvale, or The Ridge Diner, 125 Kinderkamack Road in Park Ridge, at 10 p.m. for some food and/or drinks up until midnight.**\*\*\*

About the group: We're not professionals and we're not in a league. In fact, we're a group of people who probably each won't score more than 100 points and will land more than our fair share of gutter balls. There's no pressure at all!!!! We're not here to analyze your form or technique.

More event details: You pay for your own games, food and drinks. Please bring cash. The alley does not accept card. But there is an ATM on site. The **cost per player is $18 for two games** and bowling shoes. If you have your own shoes, the cost is $12. Please try to bring exact change since Andy will have a limited amount of cash. If you bring a $20 bill and Andy doesn't have change, any remainder would just go toward the bowling alley tip.

Food and drinks (alcoholic and non-alcoholic) are available on site, plus tables and an alley bar to hang at during the event. There is a dart board and a few arcade games, as well.

**What do you do when you get there?** First, find the group inside around the front desk. Look for a growing group of people! Call or text Andy Milone at 201-234-9609 if you can't find us. Pay Andy before getting bowling shoes. **\*NEW\*** He'll give you a name tag or wrist band. **\*NEW\***

**Please know that it is possible we have to wait 20-40 minutes until lane(s) free up. (He is thinking about changes to possibly address this too).** It's certainly possible that we may have to relocate or cancel that night if there's a long wait list upon arrival. Please keep that in mind if traveling from far away!

For those who don't have cars or are looking to avoid driving, the Montvale Train Station is only like a five-minute walk from the bowling alley and can be reached via the Pascack Valley Line. Last train leaves at 12:20 a.m. and heads toward Hoboken. (https://gmtma.org/wp-content/uploads/2011/11/rail_system_map.jpg)

Many thanks to Taylor, one of our group participants, for recommending Montvale Lanes! You can learn more at: www.montvalelanes.com.

Hope you'll join us for our 22nd bowling event, or consider attending one of our volleyball, ping pong, skating or other activities -> www.meetup.com/north-jersey-bowling-soccer-volleyball
source: Meetup: NJ Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis & More

title: Good Friday Worship of Tenebrae, 7:30pm
description: The word "tenebrae" comes from the Latin word meaning"shadows."  On this night we confront the shadow side of our faith.  Through contemplative sacred music, scripture story, reflection and  hymns we will share the story of Jesus' trial, crucifixion and death.  As the light grows dimmer we are invited to stand at the foot of the cross and share this powerful experience.  You are asked to wear black clothing to this service.

title: Jazz film screening: Soundtrack to a Coup d'Etat
location: Montclair Main - Auditorium, Montclair Public Library
description: Johan Grimonprez's 2025 Oscar nominee for Best Documentary Feature dives deep into the complex history of the Democratic Republic of Congo, exploring the turbulent events behind the rise and fall of Patrice Lumumba, its first elected prime minister. From his 1961 assassination to Max Roach and Abbey Lincoln crashing the UN Security Council, Soundtrack to a Coup d'Etat reveals the covert dynamics of power and resistance that shaped Congo and the world stage. Powerful and moving.

title: SMC Trail Work Volunteer Day
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
**TRAIL WORK** will begin this year with finishing work on the Lenape Trail reroute below the Beech Brook Cascades. The steps are in (see photo), but final tread improvements on switchbacks and throughout are needed. We will meet at the Dog Park (11 Bear Lane) and head down to the Lenape trail from there.

Trail work is physically challenging but very satisfying as we use hand tools to build and repair trails. Participants must be 15+ years. Those younger than 18 must be accompanied by an adult. Participants should wear long pants due to [ticks](https://www.somocon.org/?page_id=193928&fb) and sturdy shoes, and bring gloves (though backup gloves will be available). Tools, water, and a snack will be provided. (Registration is not required though if you sign up on Meetup, it helps us know how many tools to bring.)

title: BFA Visual Communications Design Graduate Exhibition
description:
The Department of Art & Design invites you to From the Basement, the BFA Visual Communications Design 2026 Graduate Exhibition. Join us at the Finley Gallery from March 25 to April 7 to experience a dynamic showcase of work by graduating VCDS students, featuring posters, motion graphics, packaging, editorial, and more. With the opening reception on Friday, March 27, 4 to 6pm.
 
Venture into the world of graphic design at the VCDS 2026 graduate exhibition, From the Basement. This exhibition highlights the work of graduating Visual Communication Design students and the projects they developed throughout their time in the VCDS BFA program. A range of mediums will be featured, including posters, motion graphics, packaging, editorial, and more! Together, these works reflect the unique styles and creative voices of the class.

title: Make a Peacock Mandala NEW
location: Montclair Main - Conference Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register on the Adult School website.

A mandala (Sanskrit for “circle”) is an artistic representation of higher thought and deeper meaning expressed as a geometric symbol and used to focus one's attention. The peacock is the national bird of India, and in this class, participants will learn how to use acrylic paint, rods and metal balls of different sizes, and tiny mirrors on a 10" wooden round to create a beautiful, dimensional image of the bird. All materials included.

title: Mah Jongg Self-Paced Learning Space - Weekly Monday Morning - NEW
description:
The Mah Jongg Self-Paced Learning Space provides a weekly opportunity for teaching yourself the basics of American Mah Jongg at your own pace.

The library will provide the book Beginner's Guide to American Mah Jongg by Elaine Sandberg, Mah Jongg card, a set and table!  You can also boost your learning with a list of  Mah Jongg resources available at the library. 

After you know the basics of Mah Jongg, and would like to play the game with other Mah Jongg players, please register for the "Mah Jongg Meetup" by clicking here.

title: Mah Jongg: Open Play
location: Montclair Main - Cafe Area, Montclair Public Library
description:
PLEASE NOTE: You must sign up for EACH INDIVIDUAL SESSION. Signing up for one session does not register you for any following classes. Open Play is open on a first come, first served basis. 

Open Play is the perfect setting for players who know the basics and want to enjoy friendly games, practice their skills, and build confidence at the table. We'll bring the tiles, and all the accessories.  You bring your 2025 National Mah Jongg League card and your love of the game! The focus is on learning to play independently, building camaraderie, and having fun. Come meet fellow Mah Jongg enthusiasts, sharpen your strategies, and enjoy the lively spirit of the game!

Students must know the basics of mah jongg.  If you're new to the game, or don't feel comfortable sitting at a table and playing with others at a similar level, please register for a Beginner class by clicking here. Skip dates are Feb. 9th and 16th.

 

PLEASE NOTE:


	Students should have taken Beginner Mah Jongg or have equivalent knowledge and experience. 


Students should bring the 2025 National Mah Jongg League card to Open Play.

title: Practical Spanish 1
location: Montclair Main - Classroom, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

This class is for those of you who know little to no Spanish and are interested in acquiring some practical communication skills. You will be introduced to basic greetings and practice useful questions and answers. You will learn the fundamentals of Spanish pronunciation while mastering introductory grammar and vocabulary.
 
Topics covered:


	conversation/questions
	greetings
	personal information
	expressions of courtesy
	feelings
	asking for direction
	talk about needs and wants
	where people are from
	Alphabet
	Numbers/days/date
	Colors
	Basic commands
	Some ar/er/ir  regular verbs
	Verb to be: ser/estar
	Verb to go: ir
	Verbs: tener/necesitar/querer
	Ir   a
	Negation
	Subject pronouns/difference between tú/usted
	Gender and number of nouns/definite/indefinite articles
	Some adjectives including cuánto/a
	The expression “Hay”
	Basic geography, culture and food

title: LIVING GREEN: Gardening for the Birds NEW
location: Montclair Main - Auditorium, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a free Adult School event. Registration is required. Click here to register on the Adult School website.

Would you like to entice beautiful birds to your backyard?  Is there anything better than a hummingbird visiting your block party? Sadly, American songbirds are rapidly diminishing in numbers. You will learn how to support them by creating an attractive habitat for birds with native flowers and bushes from Deb Ellis, Master Gardener and leader of the Essex Chapter of the Native Plants Society. A question and answer period will follow.

title: BFA Visual Communications Design Graduate Exhibition
description:
The Department of Art & Design invites you to From the Basement, the BFA Visual Communications Design 2026 Graduate Exhibition. Join us at the Finley Gallery from March 25 to April 7 to experience a dynamic showcase of work by graduating VCDS students, featuring posters, motion graphics, packaging, editorial, and more. With the opening reception on Friday, March 27, 4 to 6pm.
 
Venture into the world of graphic design at the VCDS 2026 graduate exhibition, From the Basement. This exhibition highlights the work of graduating Visual Communication Design students and the projects they developed throughout their time in the VCDS BFA program. A range of mediums will be featured, including posters, motion graphics, packaging, editorial, and more! Together, these works reflect the unique styles and creative voices of the class.
 
Featured Designers:
Jalen Aguirre, Ysabella De Leon, Brett Diese, Domenic Falivene, Anna Fronczkowski, Tori Gallagher, Camila Garcia, Robert Gaskill, Britany Giraldo, David Guerra, Audrey Hitchens, Natalia Kurpiewski, Paolo La Rosa, Izzy Latella, Katie Mascuch, Caitlin Mendoza, Evan Okun, Erika Oliva, Julia Oliveri, Christian Pajaro, Nicole Ricci, Christopher Rizzi, Casey Ryu, Tom Sheridan, Michael Shimabukuro, Gabby Trezza, Maral Tutunjian, and Dogan Uludogan
 
BFA Visual Communications Design Graduate Exhibition
Exhibition dates: March 25–April 7, 2026
Opening Reception: Friday, March 27, 4–6pm
Finley Gallery, Finley Hall
 
Come meet the designers and celebrate their work! Support the next generation of designers as they transition from academia into the professional world.

Hosted by: Art and Design

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12352700

title: Mindfulness in the Garden - Early Spring Series
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Mindfulness in the Garden (Weekday Series) 
Instructor: Jodi Brooks 
Tuesdays, 11:00 AM – 12:00 PM 
Early Spring Session: Apr 7, 14, & 28 (No class 4/21) 
Discover how a mindfulness practice can help us awaken and  live in harmony with oneself and with the natural world. This class will include: simple instructions for getting started with a formal mindfulness practice, very gentle stretching & breathwork, and an outdoor contemplative garden walk. This is a practice of slowing down, cultivating quiet and paying attention to our senses. All levels are welcome. No experience needed. Winter session will be held indoors, with a short walk outdoors (weather permitting). 
Fee: 3 April classes: General public $75, Members $60 
Series registration only. Pre Registration is required – there are no drop-ins for this class. 
 
Late Spring Session: May 5, 12 19 & 26 
Early Summer Session: June 2, 9, 16 & 23

description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

Looking for more fun in your life? Need an excuse to see your friends without eating? Worried about dementia? Learn how to play the ancient Chinese game of Mah Jongg! title: Free Swim Lessons *Beginner*
description:

Skills Focus:

Being comfortable in the water
Learning how to float on your back and front

Introducing proper breath control in the water
Learning how to tread water
Learn alternate kicks (Froggy Kick)
Being comfortable in the deep end 
Introducing arms to your strokes

title: Practical Spanish 3: Basic Conversation
location: Montclair Main - Classroom, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register. 

This class is for those of you who are able to communicate in Spanish at a novice level, but want to continue to improve your pronunciation and your knowledge of the language while increasing your vocabulary. Presentations, conversations and dialogues will be used as techniques for oral practice. Grammatical structures will be introduced and reviewed as needed.
 
Topics covered:


	Music/songs/artists
	Cooking/Food description/Meals
	Family
	Daily routine with reflexive verbs/ accessories
	Past tense (preterite) of regular verbs and some irregular verbs
	Health and Well Being/Parts of Body
	Professions/Workplace
	Clothing/Shopping


Skip date: No class on April 28, 2026

title: Advanced Woodworking
location: Montclair - Montclair High School, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

For those with previous woodworking experience, this class will allow students to learn more advanced techniques. Students will have the opportunity to start a new project or bring in a project from home that they would like to work on.

This course will cover:
- Advanced use of saws, drills, routers, kreg jigs, dowel jigs, biscuit jointers, table saw, router table
- Various types of joinery, fasteners, and finishing
- Different edge finishes on router table
- Carving tools
- Corner clamps, bar clamps

PREREQUISITE: Completion of Woodworking 101.

Materials fee is dependent on project and payable to instructor at the beginning of class.
Small Shelves / Spice Rack / Record Storage Box: $20-$25
Shelves / End Tables / Night Stands / Stools: $60-75
Coffee Table: $100

title: Open Book / Open Mind, Nicholas Lemann, "Returning: A Search for Home Across Three Centuries"
location: Montclair Main - Auditorium, Montclair Main - Conference Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
Nicholas Lemann, former dean of Columbia Journalism School and staff writer for The New Yorker, will talk to journalist and author Dale Russakoff  ("The Prize") about about his new starkly honest family history, "Returning," in which he traces his Jewish Southern ancestors from Louisiana to Germany and explores their stories from peddlars to plantation owners (and enslavers) as well as shopkeepers and professionals. "Returning" is also a memoir of coming to terms with identity, assimilation and religious observance. Co-presented by Bnai Keshet, Congregation Shomrei Emunah, Temple Ner Tamid and Temple Sholom of West Essex.

"What does it mean to belong? 'Returning' offers a profound mediation on family, Jewish identity, and the meaning of home in a world constantly shaken by economic, social, and cultural change."― Henry Louis Gates Jr.



Doors open at 6 p.m. The discussion will be followed by an audience Q&A and book sales and signing with Watchung Booksellers. 

Open Book / Open Mind is sponsored by Montclair Public Library Foundation, Watchung Booksellers, Anonymous, Dr. Alex and Doris Malaspina, David and Mary Lee Jones, and our individual Underwriters. We are also grateful for the generous support of our in-kind sponsors, First Congregational Church of Montclair, The George, and Amanti Vino. To support Open Book / Open Mind and other library programs, click here to donate.

title: 2010's DIY Nostalgia Night
description:
Throw it back to the days of glitter, bright colors, and the ultimate DIY era at 2010’s DIY Nostalgia Night! Recreate iconic favorites like Galaxy Slime, mixing shimmering glitter and bold hues for that classic space-vibe stretch. Build your own Galaxy Jar, layering dreamy colors and cotton clouds just like the crafts that once ruled YouTube. Then jump into the fun of Finger Loom Bands, making colorful bracelets just like the ones everyone traded and stacked in the early 2010s.

title: Awakening from Grief
description:
Awakening from Grief: Finding the Way back to Joy 
April 7th & 14th, 7:00 PM – 9:30 PM 
Instructor: John Welshons 
Van Vleck House is pleased to present AWAKENING FROM GRIEF: Finding the Way Back to Joy – one of our most popular workshops. Led by John E. Welshons, and based on his critically acclaimed book of the same title, AWAKENING FROM GRIEF offers the opportunity to explore – and heal – the psychological, emotional and spiritual challenges that follow in the wake of a loss or unexpected change. This workshop helps participants to discover the inner resources that will aid them in working through life’s inevitable challenges. We will gather in the living room of the Van Vleck Main House. Our time together will combine lecture, discussion, and a variety of meditative exercises. Participants complete the workshop feeling a profound sense of relief . . . and hope. Reading Awakening From Grief prior to the class is recommended, though not a requirement. 
Fee: Series (includes 2 classes) $65 for Members $80 for General Public 
Pre Registration is required by Monday, April 6th.  There are no drop-ins for this class.

title: Awakening Awareness: Embracing This Moment
description:
The Mindful Stream
Feel free to join as we get still and practise presence within this moment. We will discuss and observe the obstacles that, seemingly, block us from being present and bring awareness to our unconsciousness in a safe, inclusive and affirming way.

Sessions are suitable for newcomers, those experienced in Tolle's teaching and anyone wishing to remain completely anonymous.

See you there!

Please be aware that sessions will close if no members have joined after 10 minutes.

Emrys has been passionate about mindfulness since 2010 and since 2021 he has been organising free, weekly online meditation groups. For more information on his free Work of Byron Katie online mediation group, visit [this Meetup Group link](https://www.meetup.com/thework-runsfree/).

title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

description:

Throughout history, there have been waves of invaders whose objective was to control this vital area. We will explore what makes this area so unique and vital by investigating the Ice Age, prehistory, invasions, migrations, language, landforms, coastline, and modern developments as well.

title: The DJ Academy: Music Matters 101
description:
DJing is one of the original elements of Hip Hop and is often considered the first. Hip Hop began in the Bronx during the 1970s at community block parties, where DJs used turntables to bring people together through music. Early pioneers like DJ Kool Herc introduced breakbeats by looping the most rhythmic parts of records, creating the foundation for breaking and shaping how dancers and MCs interacted with the music.
As Hip Hop evolved, DJing became the musical backbone of the culture. DJs provide the beats for MCing, set the tempo for dancers, and control the energy of Hip Hop spaces by reading the crowd and responding in real time. Through techniques like scratching, cutting, and beat juggling, DJs helped define Hip Hop’s sound while preserving its history and passing down cultural knowledge.
This class introduces the basics of DJing and how music shapes the dance experience. DJ Bascis will explain core concepts such as tempo, rhythm, and song structure, helping students understand how music guides movement and energy on the dance floor.
Participants will learn how DJs choose music, create smooth transitions, and adjust the vibe based on the room. 
Through simple examples and live demonstrations, students will build confidence in listening to music with intention. This can bewhether they are dancing, freestyling, or interested in DJing themselves.
This class is designed for beginners and college students, with no prior DJ or music experience required.

Hosted by: Campus Activities at Bloomfield

title: IDEALS, INTERESTS, AND INTERNATIONAL ENGAGEMENT: THE UNITED STATES IN WORLD AFFAIRS
location: Presentation Hall (College of Communication and Media)
description:
America’s role in shaping the global political and economic order has shifted repeatedly over the last 250 years, reflecting the evolution of American interests and longstanding debates regarding the role of the United States in world affairs. Drawing on foundational principles, pivotal historical moments, and contemporary challenges, this lecture explores how American power, values, and institutions have influenced international norms, security, and governance and also how international dynamics have shaped American democracy. The lecture considers tensions between ideals and practices – democracy, markets, sovereignty, nation-building, imperialism, isolationism, intervention – in U.S. engagement abroad. Participants will be invited to reflect on the ways in which American leadership on the global stage has shaped our nation and what that implies in the current era.

Hosted by: Campus Activities, Civic & Voter Engagement, Campus Activities at Bloomfield

title: NECC Business Resource Breakfast
description: The North Essex Business Resource Breakfast is a structured networking event providing each attendee the opportunity to present a brief commercial about their business and interact with members and guests. Each breakfast features a business leader speaking on a topic of interest. Attendees have the opportunity to openly network with one another in a casual and friendly atmosphere. This month's presentation will be: Reducing Your Risk From Dementia presented by Jenna Noecker from Alzheimer NJ Currently, there is no surefire way to prevent dementia, but there is growing research that shows we can potentially reduce our risk for dementia by addressing lifestyle factors. This program explores what brain health is and describes practical, evidence-based lifestyle strategies - such as exercise, sleep, and social engagement - that can help support brain health over time. Jenna Noecker, BSW serves as Programs and Services Coordinator at Alzheimer’s New Jersey and has been with the organization

title: Transactional Data from Cosmos: Powering Search, Analytics & Intelligent Exper
description:
Cloud Data Driven
Discover how to transform transactional data from Azure Cosmos DB into actionable insights and intelligent experiences. This session explores integrating Cosmos DB with Azure Synapse for advanced analytics and Azure AI Search for discoverability, context-aware interactions, and a user-friendly UI to search or retrieve data directly from Search. Learn how to implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) patterns and Agentic AI to build scalable, AI-driven solutions that ensure transactional integrity, searchability, and enterprise-grade security in a cloud-native environment.

title: Understanding Rights and Responsibilities— Immigration Policy
description:
As immigration policies develop, it is best to stay educated. Know your rights and responsibilities!
Join the New Jersey Consortium for Immigrant Children for a webinar on key legal and policy issues impacting the Montclair community. Information will be provided on immigration statuses, policy updates, legal protections, judicial vs. administrative orders, and understanding your rights if encountering law enforcement. Sessions are open for students, family, faculty, and staff in both English and Spanish. 
The New Jersey Consortium for Immigrant Children (NJCIC) is a state-wide legal and policy advocacy and education organization specializing in youth immigration. They provide objective legal resources, aid, and advice on immigration rights.
Student & Family English Session: March 25, 2:30-3:45: https://us06web.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_FxYlh_kDRkCYCoOhfmEKyA#/registration
Student & Family Spanish Session: April 9, 5:30-6:45: https://us06web.zoom.us/j/82724283081?pwd=KzAjQw24vxqmzbURBXGmbBLV3V60m3.1

title: NJ Real Estate Broker 30-Hour Office Management Course | Burgos Realty Academy
description: Registration Open NJ Real Estate Broker 30-Hour Office Management Course April 9 – April 23 | 5:30 PM – 9:30 PM Burgos Realty Academy offers the 30-hour Office Management Broker course as part of New Jersey’s required 150-hour Broker pre-licensing curriculum. This instructor-led course focuses on brokerage operations, supervision, compliance, risk management, and leadership responsibilities required to operate a real estate brokerage. The full 150-hour New Jersey Broker pre-licensing requirement includes: 30 Hours – Ethics & Agency 30 Hours – Office Management 90 Hours – Basic Broker Courses are taught by a licensed broker-owner, real estate instructor, and ARELLO Certified Distance Education Instructor. Instruction is designed to prepare students not only to pass the broker exam, but to manage, lead, and operate a compliant and effective brokerage.

title: Inspiration trap creativity workshop
description:
Live African music and drumming events in NJ
6,7&8pm Issyra Gallery presents - Inspiration Trap Creativity workshop

Only 10 attendees allowed social distancing and mandatory mask wearing.

You are invited to participate in a special session of creative thinking and fresh insight dedicated to inspiration.

We live in a world of constant change that requires us to be able to continually adapt and innovate in order to succeed. Unfortunately, many of us have forgotten how to delve into our raw creativity and leave behind the rigidity of the frameworks we have progressively interiorized.
This unique workshop seeks to reclaim our inspiration and imagination, rethink the unthinkable. It is ideal for people working in the creative arts as well as students, entrepreneurs, team managers, corporate leaders and anyone looking to implement their creative vision.

Inspiration is a vital driving force in our lives that moves us to create our best work and lights the way for new, authentic ideas. How do we invite, cultivate and harness inspiration so that we are able to bring these ideas into reality?
As a self-taught artist exploring various avenues of self-expression, Issa Sow draws a distinction between the phenomenon of inspiration itself and our task of making ourselves ready for it.
In the upcoming Inspiration Trap workshop, we will imagine ways we can invite and contain our inspiration, and well as keep our mind in the open, dynamic state as a receptacle or a “canvas” to collect, reflect and grow our ideas.
Feel free to bring along a personal object, a piece of clothing or even a small piece of furniture to play with during this hands-on experiment in innovative thinking. There are surprises and new elements in each session!

Issa =2
Issa Sow is a contemporary international autodidact artist known as Issa=2.
He believes in life being a journey of continual growth with each new

title: Raised Bed Gardening with Bracco Farms
location: Montclair Main - Classroom, Montclair Public Library
description:
Ready to take your gardening game to the next level? Join us for an engaging and informative talk all about the power of the Raised Bed Garden!

This talk will be hosted by Bracco Farms. They will discuss the benefits of raised garden beds include better soil drainage, easy access to plants, portability, soil customization increased soil control, and also the ability to keep weeds and pests at bay. Bracco Farms will discuss everything from planning to growing in a Raised Bed Garden. We will demonstrate how almost every kind of plant will grow better in it for many reasons which we will point out. They will also bring, show and demonstrate many of the hand tools used in a Raised Bed garden.

Space is limited, registration required.

location: University Hall 1030 - Charlotte Roome Shotmeyer Lecture Hall
description:
A New Member Education (NME) meeting is a structured gathering designed to help new members learn about Tri Sigma’s history, values, traditions, and expectations. During these meetings, new members build connections with one another, develop leadership and personal growth skills, and gain a deeper understanding of what it means to be a lifelong member of the organization. title: Summer Lifeguard Hiring
description:
Campus Recreation is hiring for the summer!
We want qualified staff to work during the summer at our 3 facilities on campus. 
Not certified? We can train you! 
If you work full time, we provide housing for free at the Village apartments!
Apply today
Applications due by 4/10!
All employees must be current students, registered for Fall 2026. Incoming students can be hired starting July 1st. We offer part-time and full-time hours at both indoor and outdoor facilities. We will work around class schedules, but all staff must be able to work a minimum of 14 hours (part-time), and 32 hours (full-time). Full time staff get free housing at the Village.
Currently certified lifeguards - we accept all major certifications, and can recertify you for free. 
Non-certified students - Students that are not currently certified can take a pretest for free, before you register for a lifeguard training course. If you pass the pretest, and can work a minimum of 14 hours per week for the summer (average), than we will certify you for free. If you can work full-time, we may also be able to offer free housing for the summer (space is limited, so this option is first-come, first-serve).
Swim tests will take place between March 23rd and April 10th, 2026. 

Hosted by: Campus Recreation

title: Spring Retreat
description:
Join Campus Activities for our Spring Student Retreat  a chance to step away from campus, connect with other students, and enjoy a day filled with activities, good food, and a refreshing change of scenery. This trip is designed to help you relax, recharge, and make new memories while participating in group experiences and social opportunities throughout the day.
The trip is open to current Bloomfield/Montclair State students only. Spots are limited and available on a first-come, first-served basis.
A $5 (cash only) reservation fee is required to secure your spot at the time of sign-up.
Transportation will be provided, and the bus will depart from and return to the Bloomfield Campus. All meals are included, and some snacks will be provided. Students are encouraged to wear comfortable clothing and shoes suitable for a full day of activities.
Additional trip details, including itinerary information and preparation instructions, will be shared with students once their registration and reservation are confirmed.
 
To sign up you must go to the Bloomfield Campus - Campus Activities located in Talbott Hall

description:

title: Outdoor Adventures - Arbor Day Everyday
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Saturday, 10:00 AM – 12:00 PM 
April 11th – Arbor Day Everyday 
Arbor Day is a day to reflect on the importance of trees in our environment. We have an abundance of beautiful trees throughout the gardens. At April’s Outdoor Adventures, learn more about our trees as we explore their role in our environment, their unique leaf shapes, and what animals use trees as their homes.  
Join us in the gardens as you and your family explore and learn about the outdoor world through crafts, scavenger hunts, activity sheets and educational displays. Each program will focus on a specific theme, and our Van Vleck Education team will guide your family through this interactive learning experience. This program is entirely outdoors, weather dependent. Registration is required and includes everything you need to participate in the program. Designed for children ages 4 -11 but all are welcome. 
Fee: FREE for Members of Van Vleck; $15 per family  for  General Public 
Registration required by 1 day prior to each class 
 
Sponsored by  
  
Upcoming Outdoor Adventures: 
May 16th – Honeybees & Hummingbirds 
What do bees and hummingbirds have in common? They fly and they are pollinators! Join us to learn about your local pollinators, how they make a positive impact on our environment, and what we can do to help them on their mission.  
  
June 6th – Insect Safari  
You can find many different insects buzzing, wiggling, and crawling around the gardens. Join us for an Insect Safari at this month’s Outdoor Adventures. Create a bug inspired craft, take a hike looking for insects, and learn about the important roles insects play in the garden.

title: Daytime Pool at Clifton Billiards
description:
North Jersey Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis And More (BVT)
***Trying something different this time, Saturday afternoon date and in the window for day passes! So if you are available and feel like staying longer, or coming closer to the middle or end, it's more flexible, easier to change between tables and play with more people and less time is spent counting hours, etc. We'll see how this pans out going forward.***

***Meeting Point:***
Outside of the building; After 5-10 minutes: You can meet us upstairs at the pool tables. *\*\*Please look for the group as the staff do not keep up with the group\*\***
*** Price:
Photo of the day passes pricing is posted, however if you only want to stay a short while and would rather pay less, the price is $6.50 per hour per person if you have 4 players at your table. However, the final price is based on the total amount of minutes played. At the end of the hour, the table amount will be split per person. Each table paying this way should have a table leader, **this person will collect the money of players at their table and will pay for the table on one card.** 3-4 table leaders will be needed, **volunteering for this is encouraged**

***Bringing cash is highly encouraged to pay your portion to your table leader***

*Parking*: street parking is available however it may be hard to find close to the venue. There are side streets before the town proper, but a good option is the nearby ANT bookstore which has a lot which should be okay to use.

***This location has food & drinks available for purchase.***

*** Expectations**:
Pool cues are available at the location, expect mid to low lighting. If you come after 7:15pm, look for the Meetup group upstairs.
** *What if I'm a beginner or only want to play with people at a similar level?**
Tables will be divided between beginners, intermediate players, and advanced. However, this depends on how many types of players come, so it will be divided as best as possibly available. If you would like game rules, please speak with Marc.** Marc is also available to answer questions including how to hold the cue, stances, etc.**
*** Is anyone at Clifton Billiards after the event is over?
Afterward, most go home or go to a local bar. If you'd like to hang out after, definitely speak with members to see if they want to do something after.
*** Can I switch tables?**
*Yes, but it does make it easier if you stay at your table. If you switch tables, pay the amount of time you were at that table. Example, if you were at able A for an hour, pay the hour to the table A leader. If you go table B for two hours, pay your two hours to the table B leader. At the end, the table leader is taking the payments for the total amount of time at that table.*
*** How do I get balls for the table and how many people do I tell staff that I have?*
*One person per table gives their ID to the staff in order to check out balls. Again, the price for 4 people is about $6.50. If you have more or less, it slightly changes. So it just depends on how many people are at your table. It may be better to mention that you have more people esp if you are starting with 4 or 5 people.*
*** I feel like people aren't trying to be my friend.*
*Please remember that different people have different goals. Some may come to just play billiards, others may be there to meet new people or do something social, and some may be there to make long term friends. Find people that match your goals or be ok with others that may not.
source: Meetup: NJ Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis & More

title: NJ Code & Coffee meetup - April
description:
NJ Code & Coffee
Calling all New Jersey techies to the **NJ Code and Coffee** tech community meetup at the EqualSpace ES550 Tech & Innovation campus!

Code and Coffee is an inclusive, informal networking/co-working session for those in tech (and the tech-curious).
That includes operators, founders, and investors. Developers, data engineers, UX/UI designers, data scientists, product managers, software engineers, solution architects... **all are welcome!**

People of all skill and experience levels can attend, we love it that way. Many people (optionally) bring projects to work on, and many other people (optionally) socialize the entire time. It's entirely up to you!

Come grab a cup of coffee and meet new people, share stories, find collaborators, talk about everything tech, talk about everything NOT tech, take a break from job applications, get away from the computer screen, make connections, make conversation, **make friends!**

**Agenda:**
**2pm:** Doors open, networking starts
**2:30pm:** Intro circle
**3pm:** networking continues
**5pm:** event ends

**The Intro Circle**
**Near the beginning of the event (about 2:30 pm), we will do a standup in a circle:**
We first talk about organizer announcements, updates, and logistics. Then we go around the circle where each person says:

* your name and job title/ role in tech/ tech stack,
* What you can help others with,
* What you would like help with,
* Job opportunities you're hiring for OR announce that you are looking for one. If none, that's cool.

After the introduction circle, everything is self-organized.

**Community Discord - come hang out before and after!:**
[www.codeandcoffee.chat](www.codeandcoffee.chat)

**The Venue**
Equal Space is a premium share-space created with multicultural business owners in mind, =SPACE equips founders with resources, programming and a powerful network.

Headquartered in the City of Newark, their share-space provides resources for startups, coaching for tech companies, and a wealth of intellectuals to collaborate and build with. The next wave in enterprises is being born within their walls. Become a part of the story: [EqualSpace.co/](https://equalspace.co/)

title: Croton Aqueduct Bike Trip
description:
Get ready for a scenic spin through history as we ride the legendary Old Croton Aqueduct! This unforgettable bike trip takes you along one of New York’s most beautiful and peaceful trails, where tree-lined paths, and charming river views, create the perfect backdrop for a day outdoors. As we cruise along the trail you’ll enjoy plenty of photo-worthy stops along the way. Expect a relaxed pace with time to explore, snack, and soak in the scenery. Whether you're in it for the fresh air, the history, or just a joyful ride with great company, this Croton Aqueduct adventure is a perfect blend of nature, culture, and fun on two wheels! 
How do I sign-up?

Register online through Fusion, in the Student Recreation Center's main office, or over the phone (973-655-3340).
Cost for Students - $10 | Faculty/Staff - $20

When and Where?

Sunday, April 12, 2026
10:00 am - 5:00 pm
Croton Aqueduct, NJ - Begins and ends at Student Recreation Center

What to bring:

Money for spending, lunch, water, comfortable shoes, sunscreen.

Potential Hazards: Hazardous weather, poisonous plants/allergies, insect bites, unpredictable weather changes, sunburn/heat exhaustion, dehydration, and injuries, including sprains/strains, abrasions, lacerations and fractures.
 

Hosted by: Campus Recreation

description:

(Usually meets 2nd Sundays) 12-1pm in the FCC Library
Be part of planning events, celebrating Creation,  and living your faith by taking action for the planet. Email greenteam@fccmtc.com for Zoom link.
 
 
Green Team/Creation Justice Ministry monthly meetings: 2nd Sunday of the month, after worship, in the FCC library.  Our congregation is committed to the idea that creation care is central to each aspect of our church: our theology and worship, institutional life and practices, and our partnership and work in the wider community. Learn about FCC’s climate justice initiatives, be part of planning our events, and live your faith by taking action for the planet. 
Check out our Green Team Ministry page here: https://fccmontclair.org/green-team-ministry/
and read our Creation Justice Covenant here: https://www.fccmontclair.org/creation-justice-church/

title: Second Sunday Concert: Teatro Nuovo
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
April 12, 2PM 
Led by conductor/musicologist Will Crutchfield, Teatro Nuovo – Italian for “new theater” – is a young organization devoted to cutting-edge interpretation of classic Italian opera.  Since its debut in 2018, Teatro Nuovo has presented thirteen operas by Rossini, Donizetti, Bellini, Verdi, Ricci, and Mayr, culminating in the modern premiere of Carolina Uccelli’s Anna di Resburgo, which made the New York Times list of the 20 best Classical events for 2024.  This year Teatro Nuovo will celebrate the bicentennial of Italian opera in America.

This concert will show how delving into forgotten traditions can animate the future of the art, as Will Crutchfield and young vocal stars will perform virtuoso thrillers and heart-melting lyrics from the golden age of Italian Bel Canto. Don’t miss this exciting opportunity to hear some of opera’s newest rising stars! 
Members received the registration link in mid-March. This concert is free but a donation of $10 is appreciated to continue to fund the series. Registrations are recommended but there are always a few seats available just before the start of the concert. 

 
Many thanks to our Series Patrons.

location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Journaling in the Garden 
Monthly on Mondays, 12:00 PM – 1:00 PM. (Series or individual Classes) 
March 23rd 
April 13th 
May 11th 
June 15th 
Explore the beauty of the Van Vleck Gardens this spring through reflective writing and creative mark-making.  This mindfulness-based class/series invites you to slow down, observe the natural world and turn your blank pages into a wonderland of personal observations.  Each class takes place in a different area of the Gardens (weather permitting), offering new inspiration for discovery.  Deepen your connection with nature and yourself in a no-pressure environment.  No art or writing experience necessary – just an openness to explore, reflect and create.   
Fee: Series Rate (4 classes): $50 Members / $70 General Public 
Individual Rate: $15 Members / $20 General Public 
Registration required by 1 day prior to class.

title: Community and Social Services Career Expo
description:
Community and Social Services Career ExpoDate: April 13, 2026Time: 3:30pm-6:30pmLocation: University Hall Conference Center (7th Floor)
Registration is available on Handshake: https://app.joinhandshake.com/career_fairs/37dfb726-9582-4e23-9c82-6561adbc3e11/student_preview
 
Description:
Calling all community and service-minded students exploring clinical and non-clinical career options!
 
Are you looking for a job or internship? Are you curious about the career paths you can pursue with your major or graduate program? If so, the Community and Social Services Career Expo is an amazing opportunity to meet employers that align with your professional interests. Whether you are a freshman or a graduate student, this event is designed to help you explore career possibilities, gain networking experience by talking with employers, and make connections that may eventually lead to a job or internship. There will be fun mixed in with the opportunity to win raffle prizes! 
 
More Information:- Mark your calendar! Monday, April 13, 3:30pm - 6:30pm, University Hall Conference Center (7th Floor)- Visit with non-profit, government, and corporate employers that have a service-oriented mission- Discover opportunities with campus organizations and departments, including international experiences that incorporate social impact- Pre-registration is strongly encouraged - Register on Handshake!- Event details and employer list can be found on Handshake. - Dress neatly and bring resumes! Check out Rocky's Closet for free professional attire. 
 
This event is ideal for graduate and undergraduate students seeking jobs and internships in: Counseling, Child Advocacy, Justice Studies, Social Work, Political Science, Psychology and so much more!
 
Co-sponsored by: College of Humanities and Social Sciences, College for Community Health, Office of Community Engagement and Partnerships, Office of Experiential Education and Career Connections, Office of Government Relations, Office of International Academic Initiatives
 
Questions? Contact Jennifer Dudeck-Lenis at dudecklenisj@montclair.edu.
 

Hosted by: College of Humanities and Social Sciences (CHSS), College for Community Health, Government Relations and Community Affairs , Office of International Academic Initiatives, Office of Community Engagement and Partnerships, The Office for Experiential Education and Career Connections

description:

Tuesdays, 11:00 AM – 12:00 PM 
Early Spring Session: Apr 7, 14, & 28 (No class 4/21) 
Discover how a mindfulness practice can help us awaken and  live in harmony with oneself and with the natural world. This class will include: simple instructions for getting started with a formal mindfulness practice, very gentle stretching & breathwork, and an outdoor contemplative garden walk. title: Midday MeetUp - Jews and Baseball, A Love Affair
description:
**Midday MeetUp - Jews and Baseball, A Love Affair**  
  
A monthly gathering to make connections and learn together.  
11:45am Catered Lunch (free)  
12:30pm Featured Program   
  
Join baseball historian Sam Bernstein for a lively survey of Jewish involvement in America’s National Pastime.  
  
Cost:** FREE or Suggested Donation of $10 per person to offset costs. (Bill My Account is only open to Shomrei Members)  
  
**RSVP:** Required so we know how much food to buy.  
https://shomrei.easyshul.com/events/6967e303368f110026c0c410  
  
**\***Members & PreSchool Families should use their fob to access the building.  
  
**About the Program**  
Using audio and video, *Jews and Baseball* explores the origins of the game and the impact Jewish players, owners, and innovators have had on its evolution. The program highlights figures such as Hank Greenberg, Sandy Koufax, Barney Dreyfuss, Bud Selig, Lou Boudreau, Marvin Miller, as well as the game's current Jewish stars. It also delves into topics including antisemitism in the sport, baseball’s growth in Israel, and the lore of the “Koufax Curse.”  
  
**About Sam Bernstein**  
Sam Bernstein is a retired Social Worker who has published many articles on baseball history. He received a BA in History from the American University and an MSW from Adelphi University. After a career serving the Jewish community at JCCs and Jewish Family Service, Sam worked for the Elizabeth Public Schools and as an Instructor in Social Work for Rutgers University. He was raised in Malverne, NY and moved to New Jersey with his wife Maureen in 1999.  
  
Full_Season_Schedule_for_Midday_MeetUps: https://shomrei.org/calendar/midday-meetup-calendar  
  
**Questions?** Contact June Zimmerman,   
VP for Programming  
jzimmerman@shomrei.org  
  
\#middaymeetup

title: Awakening from Grief
description:
Awakening from Grief: Finding the Way back to Joy 
April 7th & 14th, 7:00 PM – 9:30 PM 
Instructor: John Welshons 
Van Vleck House is pleased to present AWAKENING FROM GRIEF: Finding the Way Back to Joy – one of our most popular workshops. Led by John E. Welshons, and based on his critically acclaimed book of the same title, AWAKENING FROM GRIEF offers the opportunity to explore – and heal – the psychological, emotional and spiritual challenges that follow in the wake of a loss or unexpected change. This workshop helps participants to discover the inner resources that will aid them in working through life’s inevitable challenges. We will gather in the living room of the Van Vleck Main House. Our time together will combine lecture, discussion, and a variety of meditative exercises. Participants complete the workshop feeling a profound sense of relief . . . and hope. Reading Awakening From Grief prior to the class is recommended, though not a requirement. 
Fee: Series (includes 2 classes) $65 for Members $80 for General Public 
Pre Registration is required by Monday, April 6th.  There are no drop-ins for this class.

title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

title: 2026 Class Gift Tabling - Information and Philanthropy Cord Pick-Up
description:
Each year, our graduating class participates in a proud Class Gift tradition where members of the class invest in the University by supporting areas of Montclair State University that are most important to them. We invite the Class of 2026 to come together to show your support!
All gifts to Montclair count! Make your gift to any area of campus you feel most passionate about – such as the Emergency Book Fund, The Red Hawk Pantry, your school or college, The Fund for Montclair State or athletic program.
Your gift will pay it forward for future Red Hawks and kick-start your philanthropic connection to Montclair.

Commemorate your class year! Make a gift of at least $20.26 and you’ll receive your customized philanthropy cord to wear over your commencement robe. 
For a gift of $40, celebrate this milestone with your family, friends and colleagues by having your message displayed on the screen at the Commencement you will be participating in, and receive a customized philanthropy cord.

How do I get my customized philanthropy cord?
Customized philanthropy cords will be available for pick-up:

Tuesday, October 28 from 1:00 – 3:00 p.m. in the Student Center Lobby
Thursday, November 20  from 1:00 – 3:00 p.m. in the Student Center Lobby
Tuesday, December 2 from 12:00 – 3:00 p.m. in the Student Center Lobby
Bloomfield Campus Graduation Fair: Wednesday, December 10th from 12:00 – 6:00 p.m. in Deacon’s Den, Talbott Hall (for Bloomfield graduates only)
Montclair Campus Graduation Fairs: Thursday, December 11th from 10:00 a.m. – 7:00 p.m. in the Student Center Ballrooms (for Montclair or Bloomfield graduates)

Graduates can also arrange to pick up their philanthropy cords in the Overlook Corporate Center, 150 Clove Road, Little Falls, NJ during business hours, Monday through Friday, 8:30 a.m. – 4:30 p.m. Philanthropy cords can also be mailed to those who cannot pick it up. The last day we will mail the cords is Monday, December 22, 2025 at 2:30 p.m. Please contact James Stewart, Associate Director of Annual Giving at stewartj@montclair.edu or 973-655-5328 to finalize options to get your cords for the Winter Commencement.
Every student has benefitted from donor generosity to areas of academic support, emergency funding for students and overall priorities to boost students’ success every day. Thank you for paying it forward!For more information, please contact James Stewart, Associate Director of Annual Giving, at 973-655-5328 or stewartj@montclair.edu.

title: The DJ Academy: Music Matters 101
description:
DJing is one of the original elements of Hip Hop and is often considered the first. Hip Hop began in the Bronx during the 1970s at community block parties, where DJs used turntables to bring people together through music. Early pioneers like DJ Kool Herc introduced breakbeats by looping the most rhythmic parts of records, creating the foundation for breaking and shaping how dancers and MCs interacted with the music.
As Hip Hop evolved, DJing became the musical backbone of the culture. DJs provide the beats for MCing, set the tempo for dancers, and control the energy of Hip Hop spaces by reading the crowd and responding in real time. Through techniques like scratching, cutting, and beat juggling, DJs helped define Hip Hop’s sound while preserving its history and passing down cultural knowledge.
This class introduces the basics of DJing and how music shapes the dance experience. DJ Bascis will explain core concepts such as tempo, rhythm, and song structure, helping students understand how music guides movement and energy on the dance floor.
Participants will learn how DJs choose music, create smooth transitions, and adjust the vibe based on the room. 
Through simple examples and live demonstrations, students will build confidence in listening to music with intention. This can bewhether they are dancing, freestyling, or interested in DJing themselves.
This class is designed for beginners and college students, with no prior DJ or music experience required.

Hosted by: Campus Activities at Bloomfield

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12095723

location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:

January 7, 14, 21, 28 
February 4, 11, 25 
March 4, 18, 25 
April 1, 8, 15, 22, 29 
May 6, 13, 20 
June 3, 10, 24 
Join contemporary spiritual teacher and author Ramananda John E. Welshons (One Soul, One Love, One Heart, When Prayers Aren’t Answered and Awakening From Grief) for a series of evening meditation classes in the comfort of your own home via Zoom. These classes will focus on specific practices for working with fear and anxiety.  Participants will learn and practice different forms of mindfulness meditation in each session and will have the opportunity to participate in discussions of Ramananda’s three critically acclaimed books. Open to beginners and experienced practitioners, these gatherings offer an opportunity to experience deep inner peace while practicing with a highly experienced meditation teacher.  
Important Updated Registration Information: 
A Zoom Link will be sent the day of the class. If you are registering after 3 PM on the day of the class, the Zoom link will be included in the automated payment confirmation email sent when you register. There is no registration deadline for this class. 
Fee: Single class: $10 Members / $15 General Public

title: Yom HahShoah Program: After the Holocaust: Family History, Memory, and Identity
description:
**Community Program:**   
7:15PM: Light Meal  
7:40PM - 9:00PM: Program  
  
**After the Holocaust:** **Family History, Memory, and Identity**  
In the aftermath of World War II, Jewish survivors in Displaced Persons (DP) camps rebuilt their lives with remarkable resilience, creating families and ushering in an unprecedented baby boom. At the same time, family memory—preserved through stories, documents, and traditions—became a powerful means of reconstructing identity after profound loss. In this lecture, Dr. Serafima Velkovich explores how family history research and personal narratives intersect with the history of DP camps and demonstrates how individuals can uncover traces of former DP babies within their own family histories.  
  
*Join a conversation with Dr. Sera Velkovich of Yad Vashem and Shirley Grill of Shomrei Emunah as they explore the resilience, and rebuilding of lives in the aftermath of the Shoah.*

title: CALI SCHOOL OF MUSIC | Flute Masterclass with Jennifer Grim
description:
Flute Masterclass with Jennifer Grim
Jennifer Grim, professor at the University of Miami, will present a masterclass for the Cali flute studio, where students will perform and receive individualized feedback.
Praised by The New York Times as “a deft, smooth flute soloist,” Jennifer Grim is a versatile performer whose work spans solo, chamber, and orchestral stages worldwide.
A member of the flute-percussion duo Solidaire and contemporary ensemble Flagelot, she has collaborated with leading groups including the Boston Chamber Music Society and Chamber Music Society of Lincoln Center. Her acclaimed album Through Broken Time reflects her strong commitment to contemporary music.
Grim is Professor and Chair of Instrumental Performance at the Frost School of Music, University of Miami, and serves as President of the National Flute Association Board.

Hosted by: The College of the Arts

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12353914

title: Council On Ministries- Multi Platform
description:
The UUCM Council on Ministries (CoM) exists to report to UUCM, its Board and Co-Ministers on how our programs are accomplishing their particular mission while also furthering the broader mission of UUCM. Special attention is paid to any systemic or cultural obstacles hindering our ability to fulfill our mission as well as any conflicts. Its primary goals are to maintain UUCM's Mission & Vision, restore trust and relationship when frayed, and to keep all our interactions in line with UUCM's covenant. Best practices, connectivity, cross-pollination and successes are also reported. The CoM also provides assistance evaluating our ministries periodically at the discretion of the Board. Please welcome Council on Ministries liaisons into your meetings and planning when they request to observe your functioning; share with them also your minutes and other documents when requested. They are charged by the Board to help us have a broad view of our function as it relates to living out our mission and vision, so let us all assist them in this important 'meta-work.'
For more information

title: Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK. WE WALK AT A MODERATE PACE ON UNPAVED PATHS.

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.
